# Neural Architecture Search

Neural Architecture Search (NAS) automates the design of neural network architectures using search algorithms. This doc set covers the key methods, benchmarks, and practical applications of NAS across computer vision, natural language processing, and reinforcement learning domains.

Research in NAS has accelerated since Zoph and Le (2017) demonstrated that learned architectures could outperform hand-designed ones on image classification tasks. Subsequent work by Liu et al. (2019) introduced differentiable approaches that reduced search costs from thousands of GPU-hours to single GPU-days.
